# Alos HaShachar

[Back to all zmanim](index.md)

## Alos 120 Minutes

This is deprecated as a caution because it is a very early time. It should be used only lechumra, such as stopping eating at this time on a fast day; using it leniently can result in doing mitzvos hayom too early according to most opinions.

Alos, dawn, calculated very early: 120 minutes before sunrise. It is intended only lechumra, such as stopping eating from this time on a fast day, and not as the start time for mitzvos that can only be performed during the day.

Calculated as 120 fixed minutes before sunrise, or before sea-level sunrise when the elevation setting uses sea level. The 120 minutes are based on the time to walk 5 [mil](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Biblical_and_Talmudic_units_of_measurement) at 24 minutes per mil, following the view that the neshef, the time between dawn and sunrise, does not vary by season or location but depends on the time needed to walk 5 mil.

The time depends on the elevation setting: it is counted back from sunrise or from sea-level sunrise.

This time may not be available where sunrise or sunset cannot be calculated, such as in the Arctic Circle where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one when it does not set.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getAlos120Minutes`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getAlos120Minutes`

## Alos 120 Zmanis

This is deprecated as a caution because it is a very early time. It should be used only lechumra, such as stopping eating at this time on a fast day; using it leniently can result in doing mitzvos hayom too early according to most opinions.

Alos, dawn, calculated very early using 120 zmaniyos minutes, meaning 1/6 of the day before sunrise. It is intended only lechumra, such as stopping eating from this time on a fast day, and not as the start time for mitzvos that can only be performed during the day.

Calculated as two GRA shaos zmaniyos before sunrise. This is based on a 24-minute [mil](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Biblical_and_Talmudic_units_of_measurement), so 5 mil equal 120 minutes, or 1/6 of a 12-hour day. The day is measured from sea-level sunrise to sea-level sunset, or from sunrise to sunset when the elevation setting uses elevation.

The time depends on the elevation setting: the day is based either on sea-level sunrise and sea-level sunset, or on sunrise and sunset using elevation.

This time may not be available where sunrise or sunset cannot be calculated, such as in the Arctic Circle where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one when it does not set.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getAlos120Zmanis`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getAlos120Zmanis`

## Alos 18 Degrees

Alos, dawn, when the sun is 18° below the eastern geometric horizon before sunrise.

Calculated by the sun’s position: 18° below the eastern geometric horizon before sunrise.

This time may not be available in northern or southern locations, even south of the Arctic Circle or north of the Antarctic Circle, where the sun may not reach low enough below the horizon for this calculation.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getAlos18Degrees`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getAlos18Degrees`

## Alos 19 Degrees

Alos, dawn, when the sun is 19° below the eastern geometric horizon before sunrise. This is the [Rambam](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Maimonides)’s alos according to Rabbi Moshe Kosower’s [Maaglei Tzedek](https://www.worldcat.org/oclc/145454098), [Ayeles Hashachar Vol. I, page 12](https://hebrewbooks.org/pdfpager.aspx?req=33464&pgnum=13), [Yom Valayla Shel Torah, Ch. 34, p. 222](https://hebrewbooks.org/pdfpager.aspx?req=55960&pgnum=258), and Rabbi Yaakov Shakow’s [Luach Ikvei Hayom](https://www.worldcat.org/oclc/1043573513).

Calculated by the sun’s position: 19° below the eastern geometric horizon before sunrise.

This time may not be available in northern or southern locations, even south of the Arctic Circle or north of the Antarctic Circle, where the sun may not reach low enough below the horizon for this calculation.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getAlos19Degrees`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getAlos19Degrees`

## Alos 19 Point 8 Degrees

Alos, dawn, when the sun is 19.8° below the eastern geometric horizon before sunrise. It is a degree-based version of the 90-minutes-before-sunrise alos.

Calculated by the sun’s position: 19.8° below the eastern geometric horizon before sunrise. This degree is based on the sun’s position 90 minutes before sunrise in Jerusalem [around the equinox / equilux](https://kosherjava.com/2022/01/12/equinox-vs-equilux-zmanim-calculations/), which calculates to 19.8° below geometric zenith.

This time may not be available in northern or southern locations, even south of the Arctic Circle or north of the Antarctic Circle, where the sun may not reach low enough below the horizon for this calculation.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getAlos19Point8Degrees`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getAlos19Point8Degrees`

## Alos 26 Degrees

This is deprecated as a caution because it is a very early time. It should be used only lechumra, such as stopping eating at this time on a fast day; using it leniently can result in doing mitzvos hayom too early according to most opinions.

Alos, dawn, calculated very early, when the sun is 26° below the eastern geometric horizon before sunrise. It is a degree-based version of the 120-minutes-before-sunrise alos and should be used only lechumra, such as stopping eating from this time on a fast day, not as the start time for mitzvos that can only be performed during the day.

Calculated by the sun’s position: 26° below the eastern geometric horizon before sunrise. This degree is based on the sun’s position 120 minutes before sunrise in Jerusalem [around the equinox / equilux](https://kosherjava.com/2022/01/12/equinox-vs-equilux-zmanim-calculations/), which calculates to 26° below geometric zenith.

This time may not be available in northern or southern locations, even south of the Arctic Circle or north of the Antarctic Circle, where the sun may not reach low enough below the horizon for this calculation.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getAlos26Degrees`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getAlos26Degrees`

## Alos 60 Minutes

Alos, dawn, calculated as 60 minutes before sunrise. This is based on the time to walk 4 mil at 15 minutes per mil.

Calculated as 60 fixed minutes before sunrise, or before sea-level sunrise when the elevation setting uses sea level. Time-based offset calculations are based on the view of the [Rishonim](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rishonim) that the neshef, the time between dawn and sunrise, does not vary by season or location but depends on the time needed to walk 4 mil.

The time depends on the elevation setting: it is counted back from sunrise or from sea-level sunrise.

This time may not be available where sunrise or sunset cannot be calculated, such as in the Arctic Circle where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one when it does not set.

The 60-minute calculation is associated in the text with the [Chavas Yair](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yair_Bacharach) in the Mekor Chaim, with additional cited discussions including [Chut Hashani Ch. 97](https://hebrewbooks.org/pdfpager.aspx?req=45193&pgnum=214), [Divrei Malkiel](https://he.wikipedia.org/wiki/%D7%9E%D7%9C%D7%9B%D7%99%D7%90%D7%9C_%D7%A6%D7%91%D7%99_%D7%98%D7%A0%D7%A0%D7%91%D7%95%D7%99%D7%9D) [Vol. 4, Ch. 20, page 34](https://hebrewbooks.org/pdfpager.aspx?req=803&pgnum=33), the [Maharik](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Joseph_Colon_Trabotto) [Ch. 173](https://hebrewbooks.org/pdfpager.aspx?req=1142&pgnum=216), and the [Ra'avan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eliezer_ben_Nathan).

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getAlos60Minutes`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getAlos60Minutes`

## Alos 72 Zmanis

Alos, dawn, calculated using 72 zmaniyos minutes, meaning 1/10 of the day before sunrise. This calculation is used in calendars published by the [Hisachdus Harabanim D'Artzos Habris Ve'Canada](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Central_Rabbinical_Congress).

Calculated as 1.2 GRA shaos zmaniyos before sunrise. This is based on an 18-minute [mil](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Biblical_and_Talmudic_units_of_measurement), so 4 mil equal 72 minutes, or 1/10 of a 12-hour day. The day is measured from sea-level sunrise to sea-level sunset, or from sunrise to sunset when the elevation setting uses elevation.

The time depends on the elevation setting: the day is based either on sea-level sunrise and sea-level sunset, or on sunrise and sunset using elevation.

This time may not be available where sunrise or sunset cannot be calculated, such as in the Arctic Circle where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one when it does not set.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getAlos72Zmanis`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getAlos72Zmanis`

## Alos 90 Minutes

Alos, or dawn, calculated as a fixed 90 minutes before sunrise. It is based on the time to walk 4 [mil](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Biblical_and_Talmudic_units_of_measurement) at 22.5 minutes per mil, following the view of the [Rishonim](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rishonim) that the time of Neshef, between dawn and sunrise, does not vary by season or location but depends only on the time needed to walk 4 mil.

90 minutes before sunrise, or before sea-level sunrise depending on the elevation setting.

The time may not be available where the calculation cannot be computed, such as in the Arctic Circle where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one when it does not set.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getAlos90Minutes`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getAlos90Minutes`

## Alos 90 Zmanis

Alos, or dawn, calculated as 90 minutes zmaniyos before sunrise. This is 1/8 of the day, based on a 22.5-minute [mil](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Biblical_and_Talmudic_units_of_measurement), so 4 mil equal 90 minutes.

1.5 proportional hours before sunrise, or before sea-level sunrise depending on the elevation setting.

The time may not be available where the calculation cannot be computed, such as in the Arctic Circle where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one when it does not set.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getAlos90Zmanis`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getAlos90Zmanis`

## Alos 96 Minutes

Alos, or dawn, calculated as a fixed 96 minutes before sunrise. It is based on the time to walk 4 [mil](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Biblical_and_Talmudic_units_of_measurement) at 24 minutes per mil, following the view of the [Rishonim](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rishonim) that the time of Neshef, between dawn and sunrise, does not vary by season or location but depends only on the time needed to walk 4 mil.

96 minutes before sunrise, or before sea-level sunrise depending on the elevation setting.

The time may not be available where the calculation cannot be computed, such as in the Arctic Circle where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one when it does not set.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getAlos96Minutes`

## Alos 96 Zmanis

Alos, or dawn, calculated as 96 minutes zmaniyos before sunrise. This is 1/7.5 of the day, based on a 24-minute [mil](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Biblical_and_Talmudic_units_of_measurement), so 4 mil equal 96 minutes.

1.6 proportional hours before sunrise. The day is measured from sea-level sunrise to sea-level sunset, or from sunrise to sunset depending on the elevation setting.

The time may not be available where the calculation cannot be computed, such as in the Arctic Circle where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one when it does not set.

## Alos Baal Hatanya

The [Baal Hatanya](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Shneur_Zalman_of_Liadi)’s alos, or dawn, based on the sun being 16.9° below the eastern geometric horizon before sunrise. It is based on 72 minutes between dawn and netz amiti, sunrise, the time to walk 4 mil at 18 minutes per mil, associated with [Rambam](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Maimonides) and others.

The time when the sun is 16.9° below the eastern geometric horizon before sunrise. This angle is based on the sun’s position 72 minutes before netz amiti in Jerusalem [around the equinox / equilux](https://kosherjava.com/2022/01/12/equinox-vs-equilux-zmanim-calculations/).

The time may not be available in northern and southern locations, even south of the Arctic Circle and north of the Antarctic Circle, where the sun may not reach low enough below the horizon for this calculation.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getAlosBaalHatanya`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getAlosBaalHatanya`

## Alos 16 Point 1 Degrees

Alos, or dawn, based on the sun being 16.1° below the eastern geometric horizon before sunrise. It is based on 72 minutes between dawn and sunrise, and between sunset and nightfall, the time to walk 4 [mil](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Biblical_and_Talmudic_units_of_measurement) at 18 minutes per mil, associated with [Rambam](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Maimonides) and others.

The time when the sun is 16.1° below the eastern geometric horizon before sunrise. This angle is based on the sun’s position 72 minutes before sunrise in Jerusalem [around the equinox / equilux](https://kosherjava.com/2022/01/12/equinox-vs-equilux-zmanim-calculations/).

The time may not be available in northern and southern locations, even south of the Arctic Circle and north of the Antarctic Circle, where the sun may not reach low enough below the horizon for this calculation.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ZmanimCalendar.getAlos16Point1Degrees`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ZmanimCalendar.getAlos16Point1Degrees`

## Alos 72 Minutes

Alos, or dawn, calculated as a fixed 72 minutes before sunrise. It is based on the time to walk 4 [mil](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Biblical_and_Talmudic_units_of_measurement) at 18 minutes per mil. The 72-minute time, though not the concept of fixed minutes, is based on the view that Neshef, the twilight between dawn and sunrise, does not vary by season or location but depends on the time needed to walk 4 mil.

72 minutes before sunrise, or before sea-level sunrise depending on the elevation setting.

The time may not be available where the calculation cannot be computed, such as in the Arctic Circle where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one when it does not set.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ZmanimCalendar.getAlos72Minutes`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ZmanimCalendar.getAlos72Minutes`
